title: Ctenitis rufescens
intro: Ctenitis rufescens, also known as the rufescent ctenitis or rusty ctenitis, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis rufescens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ctenitis rufescens is often prepared as a decoction or tea, as well as being used in the form of tinctures and infusions to harness its medicinal benefits.
taxonomy: Ctenitis rufescens is a species of flowering plant in the fern family Dryopteridaceae. It is a type of evergreen fern native to tropical regions. Ctenitis rufescens is classified in the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, and family Dryopteridaceae. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other ferns. Further study is needed to confirm the exact classification and relationships of Ctenitis rufescens.
